---
title: "Comparison of Sample Size Calculations Across Methods"
subtitle: "R as an alternative to East and nQuery"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    title-slide-attributes:
        data-state: "hide-menubar"
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    controls-layout: bottom-right
    menu: false
    section-divs: false
    margin: 0.2
    width: 1200
    simplemenu:
        barhtml:
          header: 
            "<div class='menubar'>
              <a href='#'>
                <img class='logo' src='../SCC-HexSticker.png' width='70' style='margin:0px 10px'>
              </a>
              <ul class='menu'></ul>
            <div>"
        scale: 0.60
mermaid-format: svg
revealjs-plugins:
  - simplemenu
author:
  - name: Arocena Thomas
    orcid: 0009-0002-7014-220X
    email: arocena.thomas@gmail.com
    affiliations: Oncostat
date: last-modified
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
source("../quarto-setup.R")
ssc <- read_rds("../ssc.rds")
```

## Table of Contents {data-state="hide-menubar"}
<ul class="menu"><ul>

<!-- # Introduction {background-color="#202B3E"} -->
# Introduction {data-stack-name="Introduction"}
A paragraph with some text and a [link](http://hakim.se).

## Motivations
### Why {{< fa brands r-project >}} for sample size calculation ?

- do all analysis in {{< fa brands r-project >}} without having to use an external tool
- share code to someone (better than share an East of nQuery project)
- avoid problem with export
- free and open-source code, allows to open issue, ask new feature, fork the code

-> {{< fa brands r-project >}} packages need validation to get the same credibility and trust as proprietary softwares like East and nQuery


## Scope of the comparison
### Explored methods per clinical trials

| Endpoints  | Design Type              | Computation | Software           | {{< fa brands r-project >}} package|
|:-----------|:-------------------------|:------------|:-------------------|:---------------------------------|
| Survival   | Two-arm Fixed            |             | East, nQuery       | Rpact, Rashnu, gsDesign2         |
| Survival   | One-arm Fixed            |             | nQuery             | oa2s, sssas, Rashnu              |
| Survival   | Two-arm Group-Sequential |             | East               | Rpact, gsDesign2                 |
| Binary     | Two-arm Fixed            | Exact       | East, nQuery       | bbssr                            |
| Binary     | Two-arm Fixed            | Pooled      | East, nQuery       | Rpact, bbssr                     |
| Binary     | Two-arm Fixed            | Unpooled    | East, nQuery       |                                  |
| Binary     | One-arm Fixed            | Exact       | East               | A'Hern(no package)               |
| Binary     | One-arm Fixed            | Non_exact?  | East, nQuery       | Rpact                            |
| Binary     | Two-arm Group-Sequential | Pooled      | East               | Rpact                            |

## Scope of the comparison
### Explored clinical trials
```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 10
---
config:
  theme: neutral
---
flowchart TB
  A[Clinical Trial Design] --> AA[Time-to-Event Endpoints]
    AA --> AAA[Two-Arms]
      AAA --> AAAA([Fixed Design])
      AAA --> AAAB([Group-Sequential Design])
    AA --> AAB([One Arm])
  A --> AB[Binary Endpoints]
    AB --> ABA[Two-Arms]
      ABA --> ABAA[Fixed Design]
        ABAA --> ABAAA([Exact Computation])
        ABAA --> ABAAB([Z-pooled])
        ABAA --> ABAAC([Z-unpooled])
      ABA --> ABAB[Group-Sequential Design]
        ABAB --> ABABA([Z-pooled])
    AB --> ABB[One-Arm]
      ABB --> ABBA([Exact Computation])
      ABB --> ABBB([Non-exact Computation])
```

# Binary endpoints {data-stack-name="Binary"}

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 10
---
config:
  theme: neutral
---
flowchart TB
  AB[Binary Endpoints] --> ABA[Two-Arms]
      ABA --> ABAA[Fixed Design]
        ABAA --> ABAAA([Exact Computation])
        ABAA --> ABAAB([Z-pooled])
        ABAA --> ABAAC([Z-unpooled])
      ABA --> ABAB[Group-Sequential Design]
        ABAB --> ABABA([Z-pooled])
    AB --> ABB[One-Arm]
      ABB --> ABBA([Exact Computation])
      ABB --> ABBB([Non-exact Computation])
```

## Quick theorical background {auto-animate=true}
### Required input

- To compute required number of event we need:
  - $\alpha$: Type I error
  - power ($1-\beta$): jsp
  - Proportion of control group $\pi_c$
  - Proportion of experimental group $\pi_e$

## Quick theorical background
### formula
[Pooled/Unpooled Z-test](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Two-proportion_Z-test)
[Fisher Exact](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fisher%27s_exact_test)

---

## Two-Arms fixed design
### Methods compared

| Endpoints  | Design Type              | Computation | Software           | {{< fa brands r-project >}} package|
|:-----------|:-------------------------|:------------|:-------------------|:---------------------------------|
| Binary     | Two-arm Fixed            | Exact       | East, nQuery       | bbssr                            |
| Binary     | Two-arm Fixed            | Pooled      | East, nQuery       | Rpact, (bbssr)                   |
| Binary     | Two-arm Fixed            | Unpooled    | East, nQuery       |                                  |


::: {style="font-size: 70%;"}

- East: Difference of Proportions test [PN-2S-DI]
- nQuery : PTT36 / Inequality Tests for Difference of Two Proportions

:::

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
bbssr::BinarySampleSize()
rpact::getSampleSizeRates()
```

## Two-Arms fixed design
### Results

```{r}
#| echo: false
bin_fixed_exact <- ssc$bin$fixed_exact$combined
bin_fixed_pooled <- ssc$bin$fixed_pooled$combined
tbl_exact <- get_tbl(bin_fixed_exact)
tbl_pooled <- get_tbl(bin_fixed_pooled)
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
n_ratios_pooled <-
  tbl_pooled |> 
  drop_na(n_east) |> 
  mutate(
    rpact = n_rpact/n_east,
    nquery = n_nquery/n_east,
  ) |> 
  select(-c("n_rpact", "n_nquery", "n_bbssr")) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("rpact", "nquery"),
    names_to = "method",
    values_to = "n_ratio"
  )

n_ratios_exact <-
  tbl_exact |> 
  drop_na(n_east) |> 
  mutate(
    bbssr = n_bbssr/n_east,
    nquery = n_nquery/n_east,
  ) |> 
  select(-c("n_nquery", "n_bbssr")) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("bbssr", "nquery"),
    names_to = "method",
    values_to = "n_ratio"
  )
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
gt_ratio_pooled <-
  n_ratios_pooled |> 
  group_by(method)  |> 
  summarise(
    Min = min(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q1 = quantile(n_ratio, probs = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Moyenne = mean(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Médiane = median(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Q3 = quantile(n_ratio, probs = 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max = max(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ signif(., digit = 3))) |> 
  gt(rowname_col = c("method")) |> 
  tab_header(
    title = "Proportions des sample sizes",
    subtitle = "En fonction des méthodes par rapport aux valeurs de East"
  ) |>  
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#9A2515"),
      cell_text(style = "italic", color = "white")
      ),
    locations = cells_body(
      rows = (Q1 < 0.9 | Q3 > 1.1)
    )
  )
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
gt_ratio_exact <-
  n_ratios_exact |> 
  group_by(method)  |> 
  summarise(
    Min = min(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q1 = quantile(n_ratio, probs = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Moyenne = mean(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Médiane = median(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Q3 = quantile(n_ratio, probs = 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max = max(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ signif(., digit = 3))) |> 
  gt(rowname_col = c("method")) |> 
  tab_header(
    title = "Proportions des sample sizes",
    subtitle = "En fonction des méthodes par rapport aux valeurs de East"
  ) |>  
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#9A2515"),
      cell_text(style = "italic", color = "white")
      ),
    locations = cells_body(
      rows = (Q1 < 0.9 | Q3 > 1.1)
    )
  )
```

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {style="font-size: 70%;"}

[*pooled*]{.fg style="--col: #8a8aa1ff"}

:::

```{r}
#| echo: false
gt_ratio_pooled
```

```{r}
ggplot(n_ratios_pooled) +
aes(x = n_east, y = n_ratio, color = method) +
geom_point() +
geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 0.9, linetype = "dashed") +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1.1,  linetype = "dashed") +
labs(
  x = "Sample size de East",
  y = "Proportion"
) +
theme_minimal()
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {style="font-size: 70%;"}

[*exact*]{.fg style="--col: #8a8aa1ff"}

:::

```{r}
gt_ratio_exact
```

```{r}
ggplot(n_ratios_exact) +
aes(x = n_east, y = n_ratio, color = method) +
geom_point() +
geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 0.9, linetype = "dashed") +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1.1,  linetype = "dashed") +
labs(x = "Sample size de East", y = NULL) +
theme_minimal() 
```

:::

::::

## Two-Arms fixed design
### Results II

```{r}
ggplot(n_ratios_pooled) +
aes(x = n_east, y = n_ratio, color = method) +
geom_point() +
geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 0.9, linetype = "dashed") +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1.1,  linetype = "dashed") +
labs(
  title = "Proportions des sample sizes",
  subtitle = "En fonction des méthodes par rapport aux valeurs de East",
  x = "Sample size de East",
  y = "Proportion"
) +
theme_minimal()
```

## Two-Arms group sequential design
### Methods compared

| Endpoints  | Design Type              | Computation | Software           | {{< fa brands r-project >}} package|
|:-----------|:-------------------------|:------------|:-------------------|:---------------------------------|
| Survival   | Two-arm Group-Sequential |             | East               | Rpact, gsDesign2                 |


::: {style="font-size: 70%;"}

- East: Log Rank Test Given Accrual Duration and Study Duration [SU-2S-LRSD]

:::

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
rpact::getSampleSizeSurvival()
gsDesign2::gs_design_ahr() # needs to define enroll_rate, fail_rate before
```

## Two-Arms group sequential design
### Results I

```{r}
#| echo: false
surv_gs <- read_rds("../surv_gs.rds")
tbl <- get_tbl(surv_gs)
```


```{r}
#| echo: false
n_ratios <-
  tbl |> 
  drop_na(n_east) |> 
  mutate(
    rpact = n_rpact/n_east,
    gsdesign2 = n_gsdesign2/n_east,
  ) |> 
  select(-c("n_rpact", "n_gsdesign2"), -starts_with("e_")) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("rpact", "gsdesign2"),
    names_to = "method",
    values_to = "n_ratio"
  )
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
gt_ratio <-
  n_ratios |> 
  group_by(relevancy, method)  |> 
  summarise(
    Min = min(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q1 = quantile(n_ratio, probs = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Moyenne = mean(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Médiane = median(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Q3 = quantile(n_ratio, probs = 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max = max(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ signif(., digit = 3))) |> 
  gt(rowname_col = c("method")) |> 
  tab_header(
    title = "Proportions des sample sizes",
    subtitle = "En fonction des méthodes et de la pertinence des designs par rapport aux valeurs de East"
  ) |> 
  tab_stubhead(label = "Pertinence") |>  
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#9A2515"),
      cell_text(style = "italic", color = "white")
      ),
    locations = cells_body(
      rows = (Q1 < 0.9 | Q3 > 1.1)
    )
  ) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#f0e292ff")
    ),
    location = cells_row_groups("medium")
  ) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#a7ffc4ff")
    ),
    location = cells_row_groups("high")
  )
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
gt_ratio
```

## Two-Arms group sequential design
### Results II

```{r}
ggplot(n_ratios) +
aes(x = n_east, y = n_ratio, color = method) +
geom_point() +
geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 0.9, linetype = "dashed") +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1.1,  linetype = "dashed") +
facet_wrap( ~ relevancy, scales = "free") +
labs(
  title = "Proportions des sample sizes",
  subtitle = "En fonction des méthodes et de la pertinence des designs par rapport aux valeurs de East",
  x = "Sample size de East",
  y = "Proportion"
) +
theme_minimal()
```

## Two-Arms group sequential design
### Results III

```{r}
tbl  |>  
filter(
  alpha == 0.05, 
  power == 0.9, 
  hr  == 0.6, 
  relevancy == "high"
) |> 
mutate(
  ratio_rpact = signif(n_rpact/n_east, digit = 3),
  ratio_gsdesign2 = signif(n_gsdesign2/n_east, digit = 3)
) |> 
select(-c(starts_with("e_"), relevancy, alpha, power, hr)) |> 
rename(
  "Survie à 3 ans" := surv_t,
  "Rpact" := n_rpact,
  "East" := n_east,
  "gsDesign2" := n_gsdesign2,
  "Rpact/East" := ratio_rpact,
  "gsDesign2/East" := ratio_gsdesign2
) |> 
gt() |> 
tab_header(
    title = "Sample Size dans des cas pertinents",
    subtitle = "α = 0.05, β = 0.1 et HR = 0.6"
) |> 
tab_spanner(
    label = "Sample Size",
    columns = c("Rpact", "East", "gsDesign2")
) |> 
tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#c1dae2ff")
    ),
    location = cells_body(
      columns = c("Rpact", "East", "gsDesign2")
    )
) |> 
tab_spanner(
    label = "Ratio",
    columns = c("Rpact/East", "gsDesign2/East")
) |> 
tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#c8e2c1ff")
    ),
    location = cells_body(
      columns = c("Rpact/East", "gsDesign2/East")
    )
  )
```

## One-Arm design
### Methods compared

| Endpoints  | Design Type              | Computation | Software           | {{< fa brands r-project >}} package|
|:-----------|:-------------------------|:------------|:-------------------|:---------------------------------|
| Survival   | One-arm Fixed            |             | nQuery             | oa2s^[OneArm2stage], sssas^[SampleSizeSingleArmSurvival], Rashnu|

::: {style="font-size: 70%;"}

- nQuery : SOT1 / One Sample Log-Rank Test with Accrual

:::

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
OneArm2stage::phase2.TTE() # Version with accrual time
SampleSizeSingleArmSurvival::calcSampleSizeArcsine()
rashnu::oneSurvSampleSize()
```

## One-Arm design
### Results I

```{r}
surv_one_arm <- 
  get_tbl(ssc$surv$one_arm$combined) |> 
  mutate(relevancy = evaluate_relevancy(alpha, power, hr)) |> 
  mutate(relevancy = fct_relevel(relevancy, c("low", "medium", "high"))) |> 
  filter(n_nquery < 200000)
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
n_ratios <-
  surv_one_arm |> 
  drop_na(n_nquery) |> 
  mutate(
    oa2s = n_oa2s/n_nquery,
    rashnu = n_rashnu/n_nquery,
    sssas = n_sssas/n_nquery
  ) |> 
  select(-c("n_oa2s", "n_rashnu", "n_sssas"), -starts_with("e_")) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("oa2s", "rashnu", "sssas"),
    names_to = "method",
    values_to = "n_ratio"
  )
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
gt_ratio <-
  n_ratios |> 
  group_by(relevancy, method)  |> 
  summarise(
    Min = min(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q1 = quantile(n_ratio, probs = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Moyenne = mean(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Médiane = median(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Q3 = quantile(n_ratio, probs = 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max = max(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ signif(., digit = 3))) |> 
  gt(rowname_col = c("method")) |> 
  tab_header(
    title = "Proportions des sample sizes",
    subtitle = "En fonction des méthodes et de la pertinence des designs par rapport aux valeurs de nQuery"
  ) |> 
  tab_stubhead(label = "Pertinence") |>  
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#9A2515"),
      cell_text(style = "italic", color = "white")
      ),
    locations = cells_body(
      rows = (Q1 < 0.9 | Q3 > 1.1)
    )
  ) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#ffa194ff")
    ),
    location = cells_row_groups("low")
  ) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#f0e292ff")
    ),
    location = cells_row_groups("medium")
  ) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#a7ffc4ff")
    ),
    location = cells_row_groups("high")
  ) |> 
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "OneArm2stage",
    locations = cells_stub(rows = "oa2s")
  ) |> 
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "SampleSizeSingleArmSurvival",
    locations = cells_stub(rows = "sssas")
  ) 
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
gt_ratio
```

## One-Arm design
### Results II

```{r}
ggplot(n_ratios) +
aes(x = n_nquery, y = n_ratio, color = method) +
geom_point() +
geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 0.9, linetype = "dashed") +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1.1,  linetype = "dashed") +
facet_wrap( ~ relevancy, scales = "free") +
labs(
  title = "Proportions des sample sizes",
  subtitle = "En fonction des méthodes et de la pertinence des designs par rapport aux valeurs de nQuery",
  x = "Sample size de nQuery",
  y = "Proportion"
) +
theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
dt_one_arm <-
  surv_one_arm |> 
  rename(
    oa2s = n_oa2s,
    sssas = n_sssas,
    rashnu = n_rashnu,
    nquery = n_nquery
  ) |> 
  filter(
    alpha == 0.05,
    power == 0.9,
    hr == 0.7
  ) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("oa2s", "sssas", "rashnu", "nquery"),
    names_to = "method",
    values_to = "N"
  )

ggplot(dt_one_arm) +
aes(x = as.factor(surv_t), y = N, color = method) +
geom_point() +
labs(
  title = "Sample Size en fonction de la survie à 3 ans",
  subtitle = "α = 0.05, β = 0.1 et HR = 0.6",
  x = "Survie à 3 ans",
  y = "Sample Size"
) +
theme_minimal()
```

# Time-to-event endpoints {data-stack-name="Time-to-Event"}

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 10
---
config:
  theme: neutral
---
flowchart TB
  AA[Time-to-Event Endpoints] --> AAA[Two-Arms]
    AAA --> AAAA([Fixed Design])
    AAA --> AAAB([Group-Sequential Design])
  AA --> AAB([One Arm])
```

## Quick theorical background {auto-animate=true}
### Required input

- To compute required number of event we need:
  - $\alpha$: Type I error
  - power ($1-\beta$): jsp
  - hazard ratio: $\lambda_2/\lambda_1$

- In addition, to compute sample size we need:
  - Survival probability of control group at a certain time $t$
  - Accrual time
  - Follow-up time
  - Dropout rate

## Quick theorical background
### Schoenfeld formula

$$
e = \frac{4 (z_{1-\alpha/2} + z_{1-\beta})^2}{(\log(HR))^2}
$$

Other formula "Freedman" and "Hsieh-Freedman"

---

## Two-Arms fixed design
### Methods compared

| Endpoints  | Design Type              | Software       | {{< fa brands r-project >}} package|
|:-----------|:-------------------------|:---------------|:-----------------------------------|
| Survival   | Two-arm Fixed            | East, nQuery   | Rpact, Rashnu, gsDesign2           |


::: {style="font-size: 70%;"}

- East: Log Rank Test Given Accrual Duration and Study Duration [SU-2S-LRSD]
- nQuery : STT1 / Two Sample Log-Rank Test of Exponential Survival

:::

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
rpact::getSampleSizeSurvival()
rashnu::lakatosSampleSize()
gsDesign2::fixed_design_ahr() # needs to define enroll_rate, fail_rate before
```

## Two-Arms fixed design
### Results

```{r}
#| echo: false
surv_fixed <- ssc$surv$fixed$combined
params <- get_params(surv_fixed)
tbl <- get_tbl(surv_fixed)
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
n_ratios <-
  tbl |> 
  drop_na(n_east) |> 
  mutate(
    rpact = n_rpact/n_east,
    nquery = n_nquery/n_east,
    gsdesign2 = n_gsdesign2/n_east,
    rashnu = n_rashnu/n_east
  ) |> 
  select(-c("n_rpact", "n_nquery", "n_rashnu", "n_gsdesign2"), -starts_with("e_")) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("rpact", "nquery", "gsdesign2", "rashnu"),
    names_to = "method",
    values_to = "n_ratio"
  )
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
gt_ratio <-
  n_ratios |> 
  group_by(relevancy, method)  |> 
  summarise(
    Min = min(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q1 = quantile(n_ratio, probs = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Moyenne = mean(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Médiane = median(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Q3 = quantile(n_ratio, probs = 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max = max(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ signif(., digit = 3))) |> 
  gt(rowname_col = c("method")) |> 
  tab_header(
    title = "Proportions des sample sizes",
    subtitle = "En fonction des méthodes et de la pertinence des designs par rapport aux valeurs de East"
  ) |> 
  tab_stubhead(label = "Pertinence") |>  
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#9A2515"),
      cell_text(style = "italic", color = "white")
      ),
    locations = cells_body(
      rows = (Q1 < 0.9 | Q3 > 1.1)
    )
  ) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#ffa194ff")
    ),
    location = cells_row_groups("low")
  ) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#f0e292ff")
    ),
    location = cells_row_groups("medium")
  ) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#a7ffc4ff")
    ),
    location = cells_row_groups("high")
  )
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
gt_ratio |> 
tab_options(
  table.width = pct(100),
  table.font.size = pct(45)
) 
```

## Two-Arms fixed design
### Results II

```{r}
ggplot(n_ratios) +
aes(x = n_east, y = n_ratio, color = method) +
geom_point() +
geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 0.9, linetype = "dashed") +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1.1,  linetype = "dashed") +
facet_wrap( ~ relevancy, scales = "free") +
labs(
  title = "Proportions des sample sizes",
  subtitle = "En fonction des méthodes et de la pertinence des designs par rapport aux valeurs de East",
  x = "Sample size de East",
  y = "Proportion"
) +
theme_minimal()
```

## Two-Arms group sequential design
### Methods compared

| Endpoints  | Design Type              | Computation | Software           | {{< fa brands r-project >}} package|
|:-----------|:-------------------------|:------------|:-------------------|:---------------------------------|
| Survival   | Two-arm Group-Sequential |             | East               | Rpact, gsDesign2                 |


::: {style="font-size: 70%;"}

- East: Log Rank Test Given Accrual Duration and Study Duration [SU-2S-LRSD]

:::

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
rpact::getSampleSizeSurvival()
gsDesign2::gs_design_ahr() # needs to define enroll_rate, fail_rate before
```

## Two-Arms group sequential design
### Results I

```{r}
#| echo: false
surv_gs <- read_rds("../surv_gs.rds")
tbl <- get_tbl(surv_gs)
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
n_ratios <-
  tbl |> 
  drop_na(n_east) |> 
  mutate(
    rpact = n_rpact/n_east,
    gsdesign2 = n_gsdesign2/n_east,
  ) |> 
  select(-c("n_rpact", "n_gsdesign2"), -starts_with("e_")) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("rpact", "gsdesign2"),
    names_to = "method",
    values_to = "n_ratio"
  )
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
gt_ratio <-
  n_ratios |> 
  group_by(relevancy, method)  |> 
  summarise(
    Min = min(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q1 = quantile(n_ratio, probs = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Moyenne = mean(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Médiane = median(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Q3 = quantile(n_ratio, probs = 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max = max(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ signif(., digit = 3))) |> 
  gt(rowname_col = c("method")) |> 
  tab_header(
    title = "Proportions des sample sizes",
    subtitle = "En fonction des méthodes et de la pertinence des designs par rapport aux valeurs de East"
  ) |> 
  tab_stubhead(label = "Pertinence") |>  
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#9A2515"),
      cell_text(style = "italic", color = "white")
      ),
    locations = cells_body(
      rows = (Q1 < 0.9 | Q3 > 1.1)
    )
  ) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#f0e292ff")
    ),
    location = cells_row_groups("medium")
  ) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#a7ffc4ff")
    ),
    location = cells_row_groups("high")
  )
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
gt_ratio
```

## Two-Arms group sequential design
### Results II

```{r}
ggplot(n_ratios) +
aes(x = n_east, y = n_ratio, color = method) +
geom_point() +
geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 0.9, linetype = "dashed") +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1.1,  linetype = "dashed") +
facet_wrap( ~ relevancy, scales = "free") +
labs(
  title = "Proportions des sample sizes",
  subtitle = "En fonction des méthodes et de la pertinence des designs par rapport aux valeurs de East",
  x = "Sample size de East",
  y = "Proportion"
) +
theme_minimal()
```

## Two-Arms group sequential design
### Results III

```{r}
tbl  |>  
filter(
  alpha == 0.05, 
  power == 0.9, 
  hr  == 0.6, 
  relevancy == "high"
) |> 
mutate(
  ratio_rpact = signif(n_rpact/n_east, digit = 3),
  ratio_gsdesign2 = signif(n_gsdesign2/n_east, digit = 3)
) |> 
select(-c(starts_with("e_"), relevancy, alpha, power, hr)) |> 
rename(
  "Survie à 3 ans" := surv_t,
  "Rpact" := n_rpact,
  "East" := n_east,
  "gsDesign2" := n_gsdesign2,
  "Rpact/East" := ratio_rpact,
  "gsDesign2/East" := ratio_gsdesign2
) |> 
gt() |> 
tab_header(
    title = "Sample Size dans des cas pertinents",
    subtitle = "α = 0.05, β = 0.1 et HR = 0.6"
) |> 
tab_spanner(
    label = "Sample Size",
    columns = c("Rpact", "East", "gsDesign2")
) |> 
tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#c1dae2ff")
    ),
    location = cells_body(
      columns = c("Rpact", "East", "gsDesign2")
    )
) |> 
tab_spanner(
    label = "Ratio",
    columns = c("Rpact/East", "gsDesign2/East")
) |> 
tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#c8e2c1ff")
    ),
    location = cells_body(
      columns = c("Rpact/East", "gsDesign2/East")
    )
  )
```

## One-Arm design
### Methods compared

| Endpoints  | Design Type              | Computation | Software           | {{< fa brands r-project >}} package|
|:-----------|:-------------------------|:------------|:-------------------|:---------------------------------|
| Survival   | One-arm Fixed            |             | nQuery             | oa2s^[OneArm2stage], sssas^[SampleSizeSingleArmSurvival], Rashnu|

::: {style="font-size: 70%;"}

- nQuery : SOT1 / One Sample Log-Rank Test with Accrual

:::

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
OneArm2stage::phase2.TTE() # Version with accrual time
SampleSizeSingleArmSurvival::calcSampleSizeArcsine()
rashnu::oneSurvSampleSize()
```

## One-Arm design
### Results I

```{r}
surv_one_arm <- 
  get_tbl(ssc$surv$one_arm$combined) |> 
  mutate(relevancy = evaluate_relevancy(alpha, power, hr)) |> 
  mutate(relevancy = fct_relevel(relevancy, c("low", "medium", "high"))) |> 
  filter(n_nquery < 200000)
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
n_ratios <-
  surv_one_arm |> 
  drop_na(n_nquery) |> 
  mutate(
    oa2s = n_oa2s/n_nquery,
    rashnu = n_rashnu/n_nquery,
    sssas = n_sssas/n_nquery
  ) |> 
  select(-c("n_oa2s", "n_rashnu", "n_sssas"), -starts_with("e_")) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("oa2s", "rashnu", "sssas"),
    names_to = "method",
    values_to = "n_ratio"
  )
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
gt_ratio <-
  n_ratios |> 
  group_by(relevancy, method)  |> 
  summarise(
    Min = min(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q1 = quantile(n_ratio, probs = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Moyenne = mean(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Médiane = median(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Q3 = quantile(n_ratio, probs = 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max = max(n_ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ signif(., digit = 3))) |> 
  gt(rowname_col = c("method")) |> 
  tab_header(
    title = "Proportions des sample sizes",
    subtitle = "En fonction des méthodes et de la pertinence des designs par rapport aux valeurs de nQuery"
  ) |> 
  tab_stubhead(label = "Pertinence") |>  
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#9A2515"),
      cell_text(style = "italic", color = "white")
      ),
    locations = cells_body(
      rows = (Q1 < 0.9 | Q3 > 1.1)
    )
  ) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#ffa194ff")
    ),
    location = cells_row_groups("low")
  ) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#f0e292ff")
    ),
    location = cells_row_groups("medium")
  ) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#a7ffc4ff")
    ),
    location = cells_row_groups("high")
  ) |> 
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "OneArm2stage",
    locations = cells_stub(rows = "oa2s")
  ) |> 
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "SampleSizeSingleArmSurvival",
    locations = cells_stub(rows = "sssas")
  ) 
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
gt_ratio
```

## One-Arm design
### Results II

```{r}
ggplot(n_ratios) +
aes(x = n_nquery, y = n_ratio, color = method) +
geom_point() +
geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 0.9, linetype = "dashed") +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1) +
geom_hline(yintercept = 1.1,  linetype = "dashed") +
facet_wrap( ~ relevancy, scales = "free") +
labs(
  title = "Proportions des sample sizes",
  subtitle = "En fonction des méthodes et de la pertinence des designs par rapport aux valeurs de nQuery",
  x = "Sample size de nQuery",
  y = "Proportion"
) +
theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
dt_one_arm <-
  surv_one_arm |> 
  rename(
    oa2s = n_oa2s,
    sssas = n_sssas,
    rashnu = n_rashnu,
    nquery = n_nquery
  ) |> 
  filter(
    alpha == 0.05,
    power == 0.9,
    hr == 0.7
  ) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("oa2s", "sssas", "rashnu", "nquery"),
    names_to = "method",
    values_to = "N"
  )

ggplot(dt_one_arm) +
aes(x = as.factor(surv_t), y = N, color = method) +
geom_point() +
labs(
  title = "Sample Size en fonction de la survie à 3 ans",
  subtitle = "α = 0.05, β = 0.1 et HR = 0.6",
  x = "Survie à 3 ans",
  y = "Sample Size"
) +
theme_minimal()
```

# Vertical slide 1 {data-stack-name="Vertical"}

## Vertical slide 2

# Global options {data-stack-name="Options"}

The options below have user configurable options. In a regular Reveal.js presentation, these can be set through JavaScript, but Quarto makes it configurable through YAML options.

::: columns
::: {.column style="font-size: 2rem"}
1. menubarclass
2. menuclass
3. activeclass
4. activeelement
:::

::: {.column style="font-size: 2rem"}
5. barhtml
7. flat
8. scale

:::
:::

## Option 1: menubarclass

The menubarclass option sets the classname of menubars.

``` yaml
format:
  revealjs:
    ...
    simplemenu:
      menubarclass: "menubar"
```
<p class="small">Simplemenu will show the menubar(s) on all pages. If you do not want to show the menubar on certain pages, use data-state="hide-menubar" on that section. This behaviour also works when exporting to PDF using the Reveal.js ?print-pdf option.</p>


## Option 2: menuclass

The menuclass option sets the classname of the menu.

``` yaml
format:
  revealjs:
    ...
    simplemenu:
      menuclass: "menu"
```
Simplemenu looks inside this menu for list items (LI's).

## Option 3: activeclass

The activeclass option is the class an active menuitem gets.

``` yaml
format:
  revealjs:
    ...
    simplemenu:
      activeclass: "active"
```

## Option 4: activeelement

The activeelement option sets the item that gets the active class.

``` yaml
format:
  revealjs:
    ...
    simplemenu:
      activeelement: "li"
```
You may want to change it to the anchor inside the li, like this: `activeelement: "a"`.

## Option 5: barhtml

You can add the HTML for the header (and/or footer) through this option. This way you no longer need to edit the template.

``` yaml
format:
  revealjs:
    ...
    simplemenu:
      barhtml:
        header: "<div class='menubar'><ul class='menu'></ul></div>"
        footer: ""
```

## Option 5: barhtml (Continued)

You can also move the slide number or the controls to your header or footer. If they are nested there manually, or through the `barhtml` option, they will then display inside that header or footer.

``` yaml
format:
  revealjs:
    ...
    simplemenu:
      barhtml:
        header: ""
        footer: "<div class='menubar'><ul class='menu'></ul><div class='slide-number'></div></div>"
```

## Option 6: flat

Sometimes you’ll want to limit your presentation to horizontal slides only. To still use ‘chapters’, you can use the `flat` option. By default, it is set to `false`, but you can set it to `true`. Then, when a data-name is set for a slide, any following slides will keep that menu name.

``` yaml
format:
  revealjs:
    ...
    simplemenu:
      flat: true
```
To stop inheriting the previous slide menu name, start a new named section, or add `data-sm="false"` to your slide.


## Option 7: scale

When you have a lot of subjects/chapters in your menubar, they might not all fit in a row. You can then tweak the scale in the options. Simplemenu copies the Reveal.js (slide) scaling and adds a `scale` option on top of that.

``` yaml
format:
  revealjs:
    ...
    simplemenu:
      scale: 0.67
```
It is set to be two-thirds of the main scaling.

# More demos {data-name="More demos"}

For more demos go to the [Simplemenu plugin](https://github.com/Martinomagnifico/reveal.js-simplemenu) for Reveal.js. 
Not all of the options in the regular plugin are available in the Quarto plugin.
